jokes.text: Should the taxation of tea rise to an unacceptable level, citizens shall dump Ted into Ramsett Lake.
jokes.setup: Reading what sounds like a formal historical decree about tea taxation
jokes.punchline: The punishment is dumping a random guy named Ted in a lake
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist premise that drives the entire episode. The formal language juxtaposed with dumping Ted creates perfect comedy.

jokes.text: Their old-timey script made one of the As look like a D, so 'tea' became 'ted.'
jokes.setup: Explaining the historical mistake behind the law
jokes.punchline: The entire tradition is based on a typo
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Leslie - she knows it's absurd but still defends it. The typo explanation is both clever and believable.

jokes.text: You were so funny when you were like, 'My cell phone, I left it in my pocket.' Then you checked it, remember, and it didn't work.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to comfort Ted after being dunked
jokes.punchline: His idea of comfort is pointing out Ted's misfortune with his phone
jokes.explanation: Pure Andy character comedy - his oblivious attempt at consolation makes things worse.

jokes.text: I talked to the other Teds in town, and they're not doing it either.
jokes.setup: Ted is organizing resistance to the tradition
jokes.punchline: There are multiple Teds who all get dumped annually
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity by revealing this happens to multiple people named Ted.

jokes.text: Here's a quarter. I own your car now.
jokes.setup: Ted challenging Leslie about outdated laws
jokes.punchline: Using a racist law to 'buy' her car for 25 cents
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and escalation. The casual delivery of 'I own your car now' is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: The Pawnee Charter clearly states that any white citizen has the right to seize any Indian property for 25 cents.
jokes.setup: Ted escalating the argument about outdated laws
jokes.punchline: Revealing an absurdly racist historical law
jokes.explanation: Effective escalation showing how absurd old laws can be, though the racist content makes it more uncomfortable than funny.

jokes.text: "Whoa, I think we should be careful when we speculate what the Founders intended when they wrote the charter."
jokes.setup: Leslie earlier said the same thing about the Ted/tea distinction
jokes.punchline: Ted throws her exact words back at her about the racist law
jokes.explanation: Effective callback that shows Ted using Leslie's own logic against her argument.

jokes.text: You, a woman, just raised your voice to a landowning male.
jokes.setup: Ted continuing to cite outdated laws
jokes.punchline: Using sexist laws to justify slapping Leslie
jokes.explanation: Continues the escalation effectively, though less surprising after the car bit.

jokes.text: I was not aware of that statute.
jokes.setup: Leslie being slapped
jokes.punchline: Her formal, understated reaction to being assaulted
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie reaction - maintaining composure and government speak even when slapped.

jokes.text: I'd work all night if it meant nothing got done.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining his work philosophy
jokes.punchline: He'll work hard specifically to accomplish nothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson line - paradoxical logic delivered with complete sincerity that defines his character.

jokes.text: It's ALF.
jokes.setup: Chris building up the reveal of Ron's classmate with 'spoiler alert'
jokes.punchline: Random reference to 80s sitcom alien after dramatic buildup
jokes.explanation: Typical Chris randomness but feels somewhat forced - relies on pop culture reference without much context.

jokes.text: It better not be Terrence Howard's tank top from Hustle & Flow.
jokes.setup: Tom asking what Ann is bidding on
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and random celebrity memorabilia
jokes.explanation: Good Tom character moment - his weirdly specific pop culture obsessions.

jokes.text: I am ready to spend an amount that my accountant calls 'dangerous and irresponsible.'
jokes.setup: Tom explaining his bidding strategy
jokes.punchline: His accountant has specific warnings about his spending
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom character comedy - his extravagant spending habits told through his accountant's perspective.

jokes.text: 'Talk Like A Pittsburgh Pirate day,' which, why and how?
jokes.setup: Ben listing Leslie's many anniversary days
jokes.punchline: The specificity and absurdity of Pittsburgh pirates vs regular pirates
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from the obvious 'Talk Like a Pirate Day' to the weirdly specific Pittsburgh version.

jokes.text: This calendar was last year's Calendar Day present celebrating the first time she ever bought me a calendar.
jokes.setup: Ben explaining Leslie's anniversary obsession
jokes.punchline: She has an anniversary for buying him a calendar, celebrated with a calendar
jokes.explanation: Perfect recursive absurdity - an anniversary about buying a calendar celebrated with a calendar.

jokes.text: Have you seen those Dothraki dudes? They can get it. Everybody on that show can get it.
jokes.setup: Donna revealing she watches Game of Thrones
jokes.punchline: Her thirsty commentary on the attractive cast
jokes.explanation: Classic Donna - expressing her attraction in her characteristic direct way.

jokes.text: "I'm sorry, honey. I love you."
jokes.setup: Ben complaining about Leslie's thoughtfulness
jokes.punchline: His immediate guilt and apology to camera/Leslie
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Ben's love overriding his frustration.

jokes.text: "This is why we don't hang out."
jokes.setup: Ann's detailed Game of Thrones explanation
jokes.punchline: Ben's blunt assessment of their friendship compatibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that cuts through the nerd explanation efficiently.

jokes.text: I would now like to share some ideas I have for J.J. Abrams' seventh chapter in the Star Wars Saga.
jokes.setup: Garth beginning his filibuster
jokes.punchline: He's using government time to pitch fan fiction
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy - using a procedural loophole to subject everyone to geek fan fiction.

jokes.text: Imagine those two going up against Robot Chewbacca, 'cause that's gonna happen.
jokes.setup: Garth continuing his elaborate fan fiction
jokes.punchline: Robot Chewbacca delivered with complete confidence
jokes.explanation: Garth's confident delivery of absurd fan fiction concepts.

jokes.text: By that logic, every time I go to bed at night, I am Wolverine.
jokes.setup: Garth dismissing Leslie's historical house credentials
jokes.punchline: His analogy using his X-Men pajamas
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logic joke with perfect character integration - Garth's nerdiness used in clever argumentation.

jokes.text: You bought X-Men pajamas? / I won them, madam, in a raffle.
jokes.setup: Leslie questioning the pajamas
jokes.punchline: Garth's defensive response about winning them in a raffle
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Garth's dignity about his nerd accessories.

jokes.text: They also had wooden teeth and pooped in holes in the ground.
jokes.setup: Leslie arguing that the founders weren't perfect
jokes.punchline: Crude but accurate historical facts
jokes.explanation: Leslie bringing founders down to earth with basic bodily realities.

jokes.text: Hop on board The Management Train. First stop, Motivation Station.
jokes.setup: Chris beginning his management seminar
jokes.punchline: Overly enthusiastic corporate speak with train metaphors
jokes.explanation: Typical Chris over-enthusiasm but fairly predictable corporate training satire.

jokes.text: You're 'Future Mrs. Tiger Woods'? / I made that profile, like, ten years ago. I don't know how to change it.
jokes.setup: Ben discovering Ann's eBay username
jokes.punchline: She's stuck with an outdated aspirational username
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about outdated online profiles and Ann's mild desperation.

jokes.text: It's like being smothered with a hand-quilted pillow filled with cherished memories.
jokes.setup: Ben describing being married to Leslie
jokes.punchline: Poetic metaphor for being overwhelmed by thoughtfulness
jokes.explanation: Beautiful metaphor that perfectly captures the loving but overwhelming nature of Leslie's affection.

jokes.text: First of all, you don't steer a locomotive. The tracks do.
jokes.setup: Chris using a locomotive metaphor
jokes.punchline: Ron's literal deconstruction of the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron - destroying Chris's metaphor with literal-minded precision.

jokes.text: The Lannisters, while very wealthy, do not possess the magical abilities of, say, the Warlocks of Qarth for example.
jokes.setup: Ben making incorrect Game of Thrones reference about casting spells
jokes.punchline: Tom's compulsive need to correct fantasy lore despite the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom moment - his pop culture obsessiveness overriding practical concerns. The specific fantasy knowledge is very in character.

jokes.text: "First of all, you don't steer a locomotive. The tracks do."
jokes.setup: Chris's metaphor about steering a locomotive of positivity
jokes.punchline: Ron's literal-minded correction of the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron response - taking metaphors literally to deflate inspirational speak.

jokes.text: Money, fear, and hunger.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining the three ways to motivate people
jokes.punchline: His brutally simple and dark list
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron philosophy - maximum efficiency in delivering his cynical worldview.

jokes.text: When people feel supported, they will literally explode with productivity.
jokes.setup: Chris advocating positive management
jokes.punchline: His typical misuse of 'literally' in an absurd context
jokes.explanation: Standard Chris character beat with his trademark 'literally' misuse, but not particularly innovative.

jokes.text: My spirit blood is on your hands.
jokes.setup: April claiming Chris killed her spirit
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic 'spirit blood' metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good April weirdness - her goth dramatic flair applied to workplace training.

jokes.text: Old things are dumb. It's like, just be new.
jokes.setup: Tom complaining about the historical house
jokes.punchline: His simplistic solution to the problem of oldness
jokes.explanation: Pure Tom - his consumer culture mindset applied to historical preservation.

jokes.text: I had much better things to do. / Leslie said she'd pay me 100 bucks. / And I had nothing better to do. I'm doing it for free.
jokes.setup: Tom and Andy explaining why they're referees
jokes.punchline: Their contradictory statements about having better things to do
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast showing Tom's mercenary nature vs Andy's availability.

jokes.text: Rickets? Cured. Don't need to worry about rickets.
jokes.setup: Leslie listing her advantages for the historical challenge
jokes.punchline: Being cured of a Victorian disease as a modern advantage
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie confidence mixed with historical awareness.

jokes.text: Get in line. / Who are you? / I'm Roz. I'm Leslie's mailwoman, and I need to get her a Mail Day present by next Wednesday.
jokes.setup: Multiple people want the waffle iron
jokes.punchline: Even the mailwoman has a specific anniversary with Leslie
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing how Leslie's anniversary obsession affects even peripheral relationships.

jokes.text: I'll give it to you if you go on a date with me. / Oh, God. I can't believe I'm about to do this, but fine.
jokes.setup: Pawnshop owner making a creepy offer
jokes.punchline: Ann's desperate consideration of it
jokes.explanation: Somewhat predictable creepy pawnshop owner trope, though Ann's desperation adds something.

jokes.text: One time my refrigerator stopped working, I didn't know what to do. I just moved.
jokes.setup: Tom commenting on the historical challenge difficulty
jokes.punchline: His extreme solution to a simple appliance problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom character moment - his pampered lifestyle taken to absurd extremes.

jokes.text: All my favorite foods have butter on 'em. Pancakes, toast, popcorn, grapes.
jokes.setup: Andy listing foods with butter
jokes.punchline: Grapes as an unexpected and weird addition
jokes.explanation: Good Andy weirdness - starting normal then getting unexpectedly specific.

jokes.text: Butter is my favorite food.
jokes.setup: Andy talking about foods with butter
jokes.punchline: Revealing butter itself is his favorite food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy simplicity - cutting straight to the truth about his food preferences.

jokes.text: Look at my hoop, Leslie. Look at my hoop!
jokes.setup: Tom trying to embrace 1817 lifestyle
jokes.punchline: His childlike excitement over a simple toy hoop
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy of Tom's desperation to find entertainment in simple things.

jokes.text: Duraflex cut-top A350s in deep maroon? The honor is all mine.
jokes.setup: Chris asking Jerry to file folders
jokes.punchline: Jerry's over-excitement about specific office supplies
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry - finding genuine joy in mundane office tasks.

jokes.text: He doesn't have any new emails. / Is he a ghost?
jokes.setup: Discovering Garth has no recent emails
jokes.punchline: Tom's supernatural explanation for social isolation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from social isolation to supernatural explanation.

jokes.text: "Oh, my God. It's true. He hasn't gotten an email in twelve days!"
jokes.setup: Checking Garth's phone for cheating
jokes.punchline: Discovering his complete social isolation instead
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that makes Garth's character more sympathetic.

jokes.text: He responds to spam emails.
jokes.setup: Discovering more about Garth's email habits
jokes.punchline: He's so lonely he responds to spam
jokes.explanation: Sad but effective character detail showing Garth's social isolation.

jokes.text: Just burn it and bury the ashes and pray it doesn't haunt you.
jokes.setup: Ben asking what to do with his breakfast food Leslie sculpture
jokes.punchline: Treating it like a cursed object requiring supernatural disposal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation treating a failed craft project like a horror movie artifact.

jokes.text: File! / Oh, my goodness. Money, fear, hunger.
jokes.setup: Ron demonstrating his motivation techniques on Jerry
jokes.punchline: Chris realizing Ron's brutal methods actually work
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Ron's philosophy in action, Chris's horror at its effectiveness.

jokes.text: A birthing stick. / What's that? / You do not want to know.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking what Garth is whittling
jokes.punchline: Ominous historical medical implement
jokes.explanation: Good dark historical humor about primitive medical practices.

jokes.text: Huzzah, I'm Ted! / I'm Ted! / I'm Ted, too, guys.
jokes.setup: The new volunteer Ted tradition beginning
jokes.punchline: Multiple people enthusiastically claiming to be Ted
jokes.explanation: Good resolution showing the tradition evolving positively.

jokes.text: He's naked, everybody! Put that away! / We're all Ted! Yeah! / No, Andy!
jokes.setup: The Ted celebration getting out of hand
jokes.punchline: Andy taking the enthusiasm too far by stripping
jokes.explanation: Typical Andy taking things too far, though somewhat predictable physical comedy.

jokes.text: It's my favorite kind of battle. Two men enter, one me leaves.
jokes.setup: April commenting on Ron and Chris's competition
jokes.punchline: Twisting 'Two men enter, one man leaves' to focus on herself
jokes.explanation: Perfect April - clever wordplay showing her self-centered but clever perspective.

jokes.text: I will pit Ron and Chris against each other. They'll argue about dumb stuff that doesn't matter, and I will bail. Then I'll steal $20 from Chris's wallet and buy pizza with Andy. Oh. and I'll also steal Ron's watch, just for fun.
jokes.setup: Reading April's seminar goals envelope
jokes.punchline: Her detailed plan to manipulate the situation and commit petty theft
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - the escalation from manipulation to theft 'just for fun' is brilliantly in character and subverts the whole seminar premise.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. You guys, this is the best Breakfast Day/Waffle Day joint present I have ever received.
jokes.setup: Leslie receiving the combined holiday gift
jokes.punchline: She already has a category for joint presents for these specific holidays
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Leslie has already categorized this exact situation.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. You're both dying. / No, no.
jokes.setup: Ben saying this is their last Breakfast/Waffle Day
jokes.punchline: Leslie's immediate assumption they're terminally ill
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke showing Leslie's dramatic leap to the worst conclusion.

jokes.text: What about Ann Month and Ben Month? / How about a week? / Fine.
jokes.setup: Negotiating the new holiday schedule
jokes.punchline: Leslie immediately pushing for more time despite their compromise
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie can't help but escalate even when trying to compromise.
